points.text: Ecolab Life Sciences opened a Bioprocessing Applications Lab in Dongtan, Korea to support downstream process development for monoclonal antibody processes across Asia.

Bicara Therapeutics — ficerafusp alfa — Head and Neck Squamous Cell Carcinoma — Bifunctional EGFR-directed monoclonal antibody fused to TGF-β ligand trap designed to block EGFR survival and TGF-β signaling in tumor microenvironment.
Windward Bio secured $165M in crossover financing led by OrbiMed to advance WIN378, a fully human monoclonal antibody targeting TSLP for asthma and COPD, into Phase 3 by Q4 2026.
InflaRx N.V. highlighted vilobelimab, a first-in-class intravenous anti-C5a monoclonal antibody, as a key asset with demonstrated disease-modifying activity in multiple clinical studies.

MacroGenics received FDA removal of partial clinical hold on Phase 2 LINNET study for lorigerlimab, a bispecific DART molecule targeting PD-1 and CTLA-4, enabling resumption of enrollment in platinum-resistant ovarian and clear cell gynecologic cancers.

Novelty Nobility expanded manufacturing partnership with AGC Biologics to advance NN4101, a first-in-class bispecific antibody combining a fully human anti-c-Kit monoclonal antibody with a VEGF trap, for neovascular retinal diseases.

Rezolute — Ersodetug — Congenital Hyperinsulinism — Phase 3 sunRIZE study results presented at Pediatric Endocrine Society Annual Meeting; ersodetug is a fully human monoclonal antibody binding allosterically to the insulin receptor to decrease receptor overactivity.
Healio reported that rituximab (Rituxan, Genentech), a B-cell depleting monoclonal antibody, has demonstrated suboptimal data in lupus nephritis trials compared to other emerging therapies.

Ecolab Life Sciences — Bioprocessing Applications Lab (BPAL) — Monoclonal antibody downstream purification — Launched first Asia facility in Dongtan, Korea to optimize chromatography steps and improve yield for regional manufacturers.
Bicara Therapeutics — ficerafusp alfa — Head and Neck Squamous Cell Carcinoma — Clinical-stage bifunctional EGFR-directed monoclonal antibody bound to TGF-β ligand trap targeting solid tumors.
Windward Bio — WIN378 — Asthma/COPD — Fully human anti-TSLP monoclonal antibody; Phase 2/3 POLARIS program dose-ranging fully recruited; Phase 3 study initiation planned Q4 2026.
InflaRx — Vilobelimab — Inflammatory diseases (including ARDS) — First-in-class anti-C5a monoclonal antibody; demonstrated disease-modifying clinical activity and tolerability in multiple studies.

MacroGenics — Lorigerlimab — Platinum-resistant ovarian cancer and clear cell gynecologic cancer — FDA removed partial clinical hold on Phase 2 LINNET study; enrollment to resume under revised protocol with risk-mitigation measures for hematologic and cardiac toxicities.

Novelty Nobility — NN4101 — Neovascular retinal diseases — Expanded manufacturing agreement with AGC Biologics for GMP production at Chiba facility following successful cell line development in Copenhagen; technology transfer underway.

Rezolute — Ersodetug — Congenital Hyperinsulinism — Phase 3 sunRIZE trial (RZ358-301) oral presentation includes topline and post-hoc data; study enrolled 63 patients across 12+ countries; primary endpoints: change in weekly hypoglycemia events and percent time in hypoglycemia over 6 months.
Genentech — Rituximab — Lupus Nephritis — B-cell depleting monoclonal antibody; clinical data described as suboptimal for this indication per Congress of Clinical Rheumatology East presentation.

Ecolab Life Sciences — Expansion of bioprocessing support for monoclonal antibody manufacturing — Purolite™ resin portfolio — Established first Asian site to serve Korea, China, Japan, India, and Singapore pipelines.

Bicara Therapeutics — Bifunctional therapy platform development — ficerafusp alfa (EGFR-directed monoclonal antibody/TGF-β trap) — Clinical-stage program for solid tumors; lead candidate combines EGFR blockade with TGF-β inhibition.

Windward Bio — Long-acting immunology therapy commercialization — WIN378 (anti-TSLP mAb) + WIN027 (bispecific antibody) — $165M crossover financing secured; cash runway extended for multiple 2026 readouts.

InflaRx N.V. — Complement system targeting — Vilobelimab (anti-C5a mAb) + Izicopan (small molecule) — Vilobelimab advanced as first-in-class IV monoclonal antibody with clinical activity in inflammatory indications.

MacroGenics — Monoclonal antibody-based oncology therapeutics — Lorigerlimab (bispecific DART) — Phase 2 LINNET study enrollment resuming post-FDA hold removal; 41 participants dosed to date in LINNET, over 300 across all prior studies.

Novelty Nobility — Next-generation antibody therapeutics — NN4101 (bispecific anti-c-Kit mAb/VEGF trap) — Advancing to GMP manufacturing via AGC Biologics partnership; pipeline includes monoclonal antibodies, bispecifics, and ADCs.

AGC Biologics — Global CDMO services — NN4101 manufacturing — Executing process development and GMP production at Chiba facility; expanding capacity with new Yokohama site featuring advanced single-use bioreactor technology.

Rezolute — Late-stage rare disease development — Ersodetug (fully human monoclonal antibody) — Phase 3 sunRIZE study completed; oral presentation of results scheduled for Pediatric Endocrine Society Annual Meeting May 1, 2026.
Genentech — Monoclonal antibody evaluation in nephritis — Rituximab — B-cell depleting mechanism; efficacy in lupus nephritis noted as suboptimal in recent clinical assessments.

ficerafusp alfa — Mechanism: Bifunctional EGFR-directed monoclonal antibody fused to human TGF-β ligand trap — Phase: Clinical — Population: Solid tumors (Head and Neck Squamous Cell Carcinoma) — Comparator: N/A — Endpoint: Simultaneous EGFR survival blockade and TGF-β signaling inhibition in tumor microenvironment.

WIN378 — Mechanism: Fully human monoclonal antibody binding TSLP ligand — Phase: 2/3 (POLARIS) — Population: Asthma and COPD patients — Comparator: Not specified — Status: Phase 2 dose-ranging fully recruited; Phase 3 start Q4 2026.

Vilobelimab — Mechanism: Selective binding to free C5a (anti-C5a monoclonal antibody) — Phase: Multiple completed studies — Population: Inflammatory disease patients (including ARDS) — Comparator: Not specified — Results: Demonstrated disease-modifying clinical activity and tolerability.

Lorigerlimab — Mechanism: Bispecific DART targeting PD-1 and CTLA-4 — Phase: 2 (LINNET) — Population: Platinum-resistant ovarian cancer and clear cell gynecologic cancer patients with prior therapy — Endpoint: Objective response rate (ORR) as primary endpoint; 41 participants dosed to date.

NN4101 — Mechanism: Fully human anti-c-Kit monoclonal antibody combined with VEGF trap — Phase: Pre-GMP/Manufacturing — Population: Neovascular retinal diseases — Status: First-in-class bispecific antibody advancing to GMP manufacturing at AGC Biologics Chiba facility.

Ersodetug — Mechanism: Fully human monoclonal antibody binding allosterically to insulin receptor to decrease receptor overactivity — Phase: 3 (sunRIZE) — Population: Congenital hyperinsulinism patients aged 3 months to 45 years with hypoglycemia on standard of care — Comparator: Placebo — Endpoints: Change from baseline in average weekly hypoglycemia events and average percent time in hypoglycemia over 6 months.

Rituximab — Mechanism: B-cell depletion via monoclonal antibody targeting CD20 — Phase: Clinical evaluation — Population: Lupus nephritis patients — Results: Data described as suboptimal for achieving disease control compared to standard of care additions.

Ecolab Life Sciences — Strategic expansion in Asia — Monoclonal antibody process development — Opened new BPAL facility in Korea to reduce resin shipping times and accelerate regulatory pathways for customers.

Windward Bio — $165M crossover financing — Led by OrbiMed with participation from RA Capital, Janus Henderson, Sanofi Ventures, Harbour BioMed, and existing investors — Proceeds to fund WIN378 Phase 3 and WIN027 studies.

Novelty Nobility / AGC Biologics — Manufacturing partnership expansion — NN4101 — Second collaboration agreement for process development and GMP manufacturing at AGC's Chiba facility following successful cell line development in Copenhagen.
